items.prompt:
Read the sentences from the passage.
When the astronaut moves, the special gloves allow the movements to be shown with the video. This simulation looks and feels just like a spacewalk.
What does the use of the word simulation suggest? Select three options.

options.text: The astronaut is pretending to wear the special gloves.
The astronaut is deciding if the special gloves need to be worn.
The special gloves help the astronaut imagine going on a spacewalk.
The special gloves help the astronaut practice going on a spacewalk.
The special gloves make it seem like the astronaut is on a spacewalk.
The astronaut is dreaming about going on a spacewalk with special gloves.

items.prompt: What happens when astronauts go on spacewalks?

options.text: Astronauts lock the first airlock door and then the second door. Then, they breathe oxygen for a few hours and put on spacesuits.
Astronauts put on spacesuits and breathe oxygen for a few hours. Then, they go through the first airlock door, lock it, and go through the second door.
Astronauts open the first airlock door and lock it. Then, they put on spacesuits and breathe oxygen for a few hours. Then, they open the second door.
Astronauts breathe oxygen for a few hours, then put on spacesuits. Then, they go through the first airlock door, lock it, and go through the second door.

items.prompt: What is the most likely reason the author included headings in the passage?

options.text: to show the reader why the passage was written
to show the reader the main idea of each section
to show the reader how astronauts learn to go on spacewalks
to show the reader important details about how to become an astronaut

items.prompt: Which sentence from the passage supports the idea that it takes a lot of training to be an astronaut?

options.text: One way astronauts train for spacewalks is by swimming.
Astronauts practice spacewalks underwater in a large swimming pool.
Astronauts train seven hours in the pool for every one hour they will spend on a spacewalk.
Another way astronauts practice for a spacewalk is by using virtual reality.

items.prompt:
Read the sentence from the passage.
Floating in space is a lot like floating in water.
What does the phrase like floating in water help the reader to understand about this passage? Select all that are correct.

options.text: One way to relax in space is to float in water.
The feeling of being in space is like swimming.
Astronauts get very wet when they are in space.
Things in space are as easy to see as they are in water.
Astronauts need to breathe underwater to move in space.
One way an astronaut pretends to be in space is to be underwater.

items.prompt: What inference can be made about spacewalks? Support your answer with details from the passage.
What does the introductory section reveal about the author?

options.text: The author is a scientist interested in how spacewalks happen.
The author is an astronaut who knows important people from spacewalk history.
The author is someone who wants to entertain readers with a story about spacewalks.
The author is someone who wants to tell readers about moments in spacewalk history.

items.prompt:
Read the sentences from the passage.
When on a spacewalk, astronauts use safety tethers to stay close to their spacecraft. Tethers are like ropes.
What does the phrase like ropes tell the reader about information in the passage?

options.text: Tethers are used to tie things together.
Tethers help astronauts to do experiments.
Tethers are used to keep things tightly woven together.
Tethers help astronauts climb into a spacecraft.
